calendar.description: House Economic Development and Workforce Services Hearing
Senate Government Operations and Political Subdivisions Hearing

general_provisions: This bill addresses health coverage and resources for first responders.
hilighted_provisions: This bill:<hr><ltbullet>defines terms;<hr><ltbullet>changes the amount of revenue transferred to the Utah State Retirement Office from the insurance premiums tax and used to pay for certain firefighter retirement programs;<hr><ltbullet>creates the Firefighter Cancer Benefit Trust Fund (trust fund);<hr><ltbullet>creates a board of trustees;<hr><ltbullet>establishes the duties of the board of trustees; <hr><ltbullet>provides funding for the cancer screening program;<hr><ltbullet>provides funding for the trust fund;<hr><ltbullet>amends eligibility requirements for the Volunteer Emergency Medical Service Personnel Insurance Program;<hr><ltbullet>requires the Department of Public Safety (department) to annually submit a report on first responder agencies' compliance with requirements to provide mental health resources to first responders and first responders' spouses to the:<hr><ltbullet1>Law Enforcement and Criminal Justice Interim Committee; and<hr><ltbullet1>State Commission on Criminal and Juvenile Justice's public safety portal;<hr><ltbullet>amends eligibility for receiving mental health resources for separated first responders and separated first responders' spouses;<hr><ltbullet>creates the Mental Health Resources for First Responders Restricted Account to provide funding for the department to provide certain mental health resources to eligible small first responder agencies; and<hr><ltbullet>provides a sunset date for grants to first responder agencies.
